research.product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager (cluster expansion, asynchronous Tasks API)
research.interface: SDDC Manager REST API 9.1 (developer.broadcom.com xAPIs, /v1 base path)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Update a Cluster by adding or removing Hosts (PATCH /v1/clusters/{id})
official_sources.used_for: ClusterUpdateSpec shape with clusterExpansionSpec.hostSpecs[] (id, hostNetworkSpec.vmNics[{id, vdsName, uplink}], azName, ...); 202 Accepted returning a Task; 400/403/404/500 Error responses

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Get a Task by ID (GET /v1/tasks/{id})
official_sources.used_for: Task schema (id, name, type, status, creationTimestamp, completionTimestamp, subTasks, errors, resources, resolutionStatus, isCancellable, isRetryable), status enum PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/SUCCESSFUL/FAILED/CANCELLED/COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING/SKIPPED/QUEUED/TIMED_OUT, recursive Error schema (errorCode, errorType, message, remediationMessage, referenceToken, causes, nestedErrors), 404 'Task not found'

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Operation Index
official_sources.used_for: DELETE /v1/tasks/{id} 'Cancel a Task by ID, if it exists' and PATCH /v1/tasks/{id} 'Retry a failed Task by ID' exist on the current surface; cluster and task operation inventory

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Resource data structure
official_sources.used_for: Resource fields carried by Task.resources: resourceId (required), fqdn, type (required enum SDDC_MANAGER/PSC/VCENTER/NSXT_MANAGER/NSX_ALB/ESXI/HCX_MANAGER/VSP), name, sans

research.language_rationale: Broadcom publishes no official Go SDK for the SDDC Manager API; the documented surface is REST over /v1 with bearer tokens, and Go's stdlib net/http and encoding/json are the idiomatic zero-dependency way to build the long-running-operation watcher our automation needs.

verified_facts: Cluster expansion is submitted as PATCH /v1/clusters/{id} with a ClusterUpdateSpec whose clusterExpansionSpec.hostSpecs lists the hosts to add; the API answers 202 Accepted with a Task body.
Task.status values: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESSFUL, FAILED, CANCELLED, COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING, SKIPPED, QUEUED, TIMED_OUT.
Tasks are read back via GET /v1/tasks/{id} (404 when the task does not exist) and cancelled via DELETE /v1/tasks/{id}.

VCF errors are composite and recursive: errorCode, errorType, message, remediationMessage, referenceToken, causes[{type, message}], nestedErrors[Error]; tasks carry them at the task level and per subTask.
Task.resources entries follow the 9.1 Resource model: resourceId, fqdn, type, name.
Errors decode from a common Error envelope on non-2xx responses (400/403/404/500 documented for cluster update; 404/500 for task get).
